case.title: ALLENDALE CONGREGATION, JEHOVAH'S WITNESSES v. GROSMAN

opinion.h: ALLENDALE CONGREGATION, JEHOVAH'S WITNESSES v. GROSMAN, 361 U.S. 536 (1960)
ALLENDALE CONGREGATION OF JEHOVAH'S WITNESSES v. GROSMAN ET AL. APPEAL FROM THE SUPREME COURT OF NEW JERSEY. No. 579. Decided February 23, 1960.
opinion.p: Appeal dismissed for want of a substantial federal question.
The motion to dismiss is granted and the appeal is dismissed for want of a substantial federal question.
opinion.h: DUNITZ v. CITY OF LOS ANGELES, 361 U.S. 536 (1960)
opinion.p: 361 U.S. 536 DUNITZ ET AL. v. CITY OF LOS ANGELES ET AL. APPEAL FROM THE SUPREME COURT OF CALIFORNIA. No. 581. Decided February 23, 1960.
Reported below: See 170 Cal. App. 2d 399, 338 P.2d 1001.
Roger Arnebergh and Bourke Jones for appellees.
The motion to dismiss is granted and the appeal is dismissed. Treating the papers whereon the appeal was taken as a petition for writ of certiorari, certiorari is denied.
